programmes.show_description: Burgers, Buns And Bagels. Guy Fieri is going carb-crazy this trip! He finds a deli serving up lamb burgers and ribs. And, a Koreatown eatery makes Asian buns and a bacon-corn dish. S25, Ep8
Not Your Typical Joints. Guy checks out restaurants with funky dishes and unique specialties. They include an eatery tucked inside a beer store serving crazy waffle creations! S26, Ep7
Kickin' It In Cancun. Guy Fieri feels the heat in Mexico! He checks out a family-owned spot where grandma cooks up rellenos. And, Guy shows off his Playa Del Carmen restaurant. S26, Ep6
Wonder Women. Guy Fieri visits all-female line-ups in Houston and Orlando. A grandma serves up family recipes like homemade tamales, and he checks out a funky meatball spot. S26, Ep10
Multicultural Cooking. Guy Fieri digs into the gastronomic traditions of different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida. S26, Ep11
From Spicy To Icy. Guy Fieri heats up with spice and cools off with ice! He visits a hot BBQ joint in California, a Cajun spot in Virginia and a Hawaiian place's tropical shaved ice. S26, Ep15
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
From North To South America. From a Houston joint's Argentinian empanadas to street tacos in Cancun, Mexico, Guy Fieri digs into flavours spanning the Americas. S26, Ep8
Turkey, Taters And Dogs. Guy digs into a mashup of flavours, including a potato-centric palace dishing out hearty Brazilian specialties and a deli serving latkes and turkey pastrami. S26, Ep12
Island And Desert. Guy Fieri hunts desert and island flavour. He enjoys nostalgic breakfast tarts at a café in California and fish and chips at a gluten-free spot in Hawaii. S26, Ep16
Chew And Brew. Guy Fieri samples pub food in Minneapolis, Miami and LA, from wild boar gnocchi to a lamb belly BLT. And, he meets Harry Wayne Casey from KC and The Sunshine Band! S25, Ep4
Kansas City. Adam Richman is in Kansas City for some world-class barbecue, some of the best pan-fried chicken in the USA and the six-pound Ultimate Destroyer sandwich! S2, Ep30
Jersey Shore. Adam is at the Jersey Shore checking out a Trenton-style tomato pie and mighty octopus-shaped ice cream sundae. Then, he tries an insanely spicy wing-eating challenge. S2, Ep32
Portland, ME. Adam Richman visits the land of lighthouses and lobsters: Portland, Maine. He samples lobster right out of the pot and some of the best coastal views imaginable. S2, Ep34
Butte. Adam tries a local staple in Butte, Montana: the pasty, a delicious meat-and-vegetable-filled pastry whose culinary tradition is as old as the mines. S2, Ep36
Des Moines. Adam Richman is in Des Moines, Iowa, for some Midwestern comfort food, unique sandwich-inspired pizza and the five-pound Adam Emmenecker sandwich challenge. S2, Ep38
Indianapolis. Foodie Adam Richman is in Indianapolis, Indiana, for the Indy staple pork tenderloin sandwich, some Hoosier home cooking and the enormous Big Ugly Burger Challenge. S2, Ep31
Syracuse. Adam Richman is in Syracuse, NY, checking out some serious grub. He tries the pork-sket sandwich at a biker BBQ joint. Then he takes on a monumental frittata challenge. S2, Ep33
Niagara Falls. Adam gets romantic at Niagara Falls. Adam has a date with a steak that barely fits on a plate. Then he crosses the border for the Italian Challenge. S2, Ep35
Bologna. Adam's in Bologna tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including Bolognese sauce and a sweet made with rice and almonds. S1, Ep7
Naples. Adam's in Naples tasting iconic foods named after this city, including the classic Napolitina pizza and a pasta dish called spaghetti alle vongole. S1, Ep8
James heads to the riverside town of Isle Sur La Sorge, where his food hero, Keith Floyd, once owned a restaurant. He cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. S1, Ep15
James travels to Normandy, home to his all-time favourite ingredient, butter! He cooks up artichokes with hollandaise whilst visiting a Calvados orchard. S1, Ep16
It's Bread Week and the bakers prove themselves in a sweet and savoury signature. There's a special technical that honours the NHS and an artistic showstopper. S11, Ep3
In Chocolate Week, and the bakers face a deceptively simple traybake signature. There's a chocolate and nut-filled technical and a white chocolate showstopper. S11, Ep4
It's Pastry Week and the bakers put their twist on a Cornish classic. They go retro in the technical and make a tart hidden within a cage made of pastry! S11, Ep5
It's Japanese Week and the bakers tackle steamed buns and a layered technical challenge. Plus, a showstopper is inspired by kawaii - the Japanese love of all things cute! S11, Ep6
Dave Myers and Si King explore the food of the American South. They start in Memphis, Tennessee, where they try iconic Southern dishes: a soul stew and fried chicken. S1, Ep1
Blues. The bikers head to New Orleans, where they meet blues musician Little Freddie King and share their own take on a traditional, Southern classic - the po' boy sandwich. S1, Ep2
James heads to the riverside town of Isle Sur La Sorge, where his food hero, Keith Floyd, once owned a restaurant. He cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. S1, Ep15
James travels to Normandy, home to his all-time favourite ingredient, butter! He cooks up artichokes with hollandaise whilst visiting a Calvados orchard. S1, Ep16
Goin' International. Guy Fieri grabs gangster global grub such as oxtail and curry goat from a Jamaican joint in Houston, and a 24-hour burrito hot spot in Cancun, Mexico. S26, Ep9
Burgers, Beef And Barbecue. Guy Fieri samples beef and barbecue in Florida and California, including a Vietnamese spot serving up tasty pho and a BBQ joint making a twist on lasagne. S26, Ep13
Soakin' Up Maui. Guy Fieri cruises Maui, Hawaii, with his buddy chef Reno Henriques for regional favourites such as moco and kalbi ribs, food truck fare and St. Louis-style barbecue. S25, Ep1
Sausage, Seafood And Shawarma. Guy Fieri rolls into Minneapolis for a family pizza-making tradition, Miami for Haitian classics and Portsmouth, NH, for global-inspired street food. S25, Ep6

Long Island, New York. Casey Webb heads to Long Island for an Italian chicken parmesan-pizza hybrid, a twist on a Jewish meat-and-potato tradition and a 10-scoop ice cream challenge. S5, Ep1
Delaware Beaches. Casey Webb's quest for the best takes him to Delaware for an ice cream custard that has locals lining up. Plus, he takes on a daunting surf and turf challenge. S5, Ep2
Cajun. Si and Dave visit the hotbed of Cajun culture in Lafayette, Louisiana. There, the bikers sample crawfish and groove at a jam session. S1, Ep4
Home Sweet Home: PDQ. It's all about speed as Ree shares four quick recipes! With her kids behind the camera, she makes pancetta and butternut squash pasta and simple chocolate cake. S25, Ep13
Ask Me Anything Holidays. Ree Drummond tackles a whole host of holiday questions, and the answers include cheesy lasagne dip and chips and a festive salad perfect for the big day. S24, Ep1

programmes.show_name: Jamie's Easy Meals For Every Day
programmes.show_description: Avocado Hollandaise/Sausage Pizza. Jamie makes avocado hollandaise, served with steamed cod and asparagus. His son Buddy gets involved in cooking a deliciously different pizza. S1, Ep19

programmes.show_name: Jamie's Easy Meals For Every Day
programmes.show_description: Jalfrezi Traybake/Broccoli & Cheese Pierogi. Jamie cooks an easy traybake, chicken and pepper jalfrezi. Then he shares a new way to use broccoli in stuffed pierogi dumplings. S1, Ep20
Buon Appetito. Ina creates a classic Italian Bolognese, but she updates it to be an easy weeknight dinner. Then, she makes a traditional Tuscan roasted sausage and grapes dish. S5, Ep3
Market Day. Ina is inspired by local produce and embraces the incredible ingredients that she can find on her doorstep. She makes honey vanilla pound cake and corn and Brie salad. S5, Ep4
This week's technical challenge is iced fingers and after seven weeks of gruelling challenges, the four remaining bakers must prove they are worth a place in the final. S2, Ep7
The three finalists must bake for The Great British Bake Off's street party making a selection of petits fours and puff pastry. Only one can take home the Bake Off trophy. S2, Ep8

programmes.show_name: James Martin's United Cakes Of America
programmes.show_description: James visits Connecticut's culinary hotspots. He checks out a diner for a traditional pancake breakfast then makes bacon waffles and strawberry shortbread. S1, Ep11

programmes.show_name: James Martin's United Cakes Of America
programmes.show_description: At a local cooking class James makes a New England classic, chocolate chip cookie. Plus, he makes a spiced oatmeal and treacle cake. S1, Ep12

programmes.show_name: James Martin's United Cakes Of America
programmes.show_description: In Massachusetts, James finds out why the Boston cream pie is the official state dessert. Later, he makes a sumptuous chocolate and coffee cake and a chocolate brownie. S1, Ep13

programmes.show_name: James Martin's United Cakes Of America
programmes.show_description: In Boston, James enjoys a classic pecan pie and visits a local rum distillery. Plus, he makes a delicious banana, pecan and maple syrup cake, and an orange and kirsch pound cake. S1, Ep14

Bari. Adam's in Bari tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including focaccia Barese and a local seafood dish called tiella Barese. S1, Ep9
Rome. Adam's in Rome tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including pasta dish cacio e pepe, and porchetta Romana: suckling pig Rome style. S1, Ep10

programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Good Eggs. Tom showcases the magic properties of eggs. Custard tart is on the menu, and Tom shares how to make foolproof white chocolate and lemon soufflés. S2, Ep9

programmes.show_name: Tom Kerridge Secrets Of The Pub Kitchen
programmes.show_description: Italian Job. Tom's menu includes wild boar lasagne, sausage and fennel rigatoni and tiramisu pots. Chef Sarah takes control when a rush of orders hits the kitchen. S2, Ep10
BBQ Bliss. Adam is in Tennessee to taste succulent baby back ribs that have been charcoal smoked for 14 hours. And, he is challenged to finish a humongous smoked meat sandwich. S1, Ep9
Savannah, GA. Casey Webb travels down south to Savannah to try some of Georgia's best fried-chicken and he takes on the Pigzilla challenge, a four-pound pulled-pork sandwich. S3, Ep5
